questions.sentence: A common problem in development is 'it works on my machine but not in production'.
A Docker container is a lightweight, standalone package that includes everything needed to run an application: code, runtime, system tools, and libraries.
By using a 'Dockerfile', you can define the exact environment your app needs.
This ensures that the application behaves identically whether it is running on a local laptop, a staging server, or a production environment.
Understanding the basics of Docker is incredibly helpful for frontend developers today as it simplifies the setup process and improves collaboration between development and operations teams.
